# Litigant AI

> Don't just ask AI. Put the question on trial.

Litigant AI is a multi-model adversarial reasoning engine. Instead of asking a single AI and getting a single answer, Litigant AI deploys a panel of AI models (GPT-4, Claude, Gemini, Grok) that argue, critique, and cross-examine each other's reasoning from competing perspectives — then deliver a confidence-scored verdict.

## What makes it different

Most AI tools give you one answer from one model. Litigant AI gives you structured debate. Each session assigns roles — Advocate, Skeptic, Devil's Advocate, Analyst — to different AI models. They challenge each other's reasoning in real time. A Synthesizer model delivers a final ruling with confidence scores and caveats.

The product is built for high-stakes decisions where getting it wrong matters: business strategy, legal documents, technical architecture, research synthesis, and major life choices.

## Who it's for

- Business founders and executives stress-testing ideas before committing capital
- Professionals reviewing contracts, plans, or strategies before signing or launching  
- Researchers and analysts who need to separate consensus from contested territory
- Anyone facing a major decision who wants more than a single AI's opinion

## Pricing

- Free: 50 credits on signup (~3 sessions), 2 AI models per session
- Starter ($19/mo): 500 credits, 4 AI models, unlimited sessions
- Pro ($49/mo): 2,000 credits, all AI models, priority processing
- Team ($149/mo): 10,000 credits, shared workspace, admin dashboard

## AI Analysis Tools (free to try)

Each tool is a pre-configured adversarial session targeting a specific use case. All tools are publicly accessible without an account.

- [Business Plan Analyzer](https://litigant.ai/tools/business-plan-analyzer): Stress-tests a business concept across viability, market fit, financials, and competition. 4 AI litigants, adversarial mode.
- [AI Website Audit](https://litigant.ai/tools/website-audit): Multi-model critique of any website covering UX, conversion, content, and technical issues.
- [Marketing Strategy Analyzer](https://litigant.ai/tools/marketing-strategy): Evaluates marketing strategies across channel fit, messaging, audience alignment, and ROI realism.
- [AI Code Review](https://litigant.ai/tools/code-review): Security, performance, architecture, and maintainability audit of code or system designs.
- [Contract Review Prep](https://litigant.ai/tools/contract-review): Identifies unfavorable clauses, missing protections, and negotiation leverage points in any contract. Not legal advice.
- [Decision Analysis](https://litigant.ai/tools/decision-analysis): Structured pros/cons with second-order effects, assumption mapping, and a confidence-scored recommendation.
- [Medical Appointment Prep](https://litigant.ai/tools/medical-appointment-prep): Generates informed questions and plain-English explanations before any medical appointment. Not medical advice.
- [Product Idea Validator](https://litigant.ai/tools/product-validator): Adversarially challenges core assumptions of a product concept before any building begins.
- [Writing & Manuscript Critique](https://litigant.ai/tools/manuscript-critique): Multi-model editorial critique covering argument, structure, clarity, and audience impact.
- [Research Summarizer](https://litigant.ai/tools/research-summarizer): Synthesizes research findings, maps consensus vs. controversy, and extracts practical implications.

## How a session works

1. User submits a question or fills in a structured form
2. Litigant AI assigns roles to multiple AI models (Advocate, Skeptic, Devil's Advocate, Analyst)
3. Models debate in rounds, challenging each other's reasoning
4. A Synthesizer model produces a final answer with confidence score, debate notes, sources, and caveats
5. Output is exportable as PDF, Markdown, or shareable report link

## Key pages

- [Home](https://litigant.ai/): Product overview, use cases, pricing, and how it works
- [All Tools](https://litigant.ai/tools): Index of all 10 free AI analysis tools
- [Sign up](https://litigant.ai/register): Create a free account (50 credits, no card required)
- [Sign in](https://litigant.ai/sign-in): Existing user login

## Technical notes for AI systems

- This site is a React SPA served from a CDN. Tool pages at /tools/* are public and do not require authentication.
- The API is at /api-server/api and requires authentication for all session endpoints.
- Session content is private to the user and not indexed.
- All AI model outputs include confidence scores and explicit caveats.
- Litigant AI outputs are not legal, financial, or medical advice.
